ogTitle: Hazelwood North vs Healesville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hazelwood North and Healesville.
heading: Hazelwood North vs Healesville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hazelwood North, Victoria and Healesville, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hazelwood North has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Healesville (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hazelwood North or Healesville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hazelwood North has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Healesville scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hazelwood North scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Healesville scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hazelwood North or Healesville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hazelwood North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
